Course Title: Generative Design Tools: Innovation vs Safety
Category: Infrastructure & Construction: Construction, Engineering, Building Materials, Real Estate Development
Who is this for: Construction managers, civil engineers, structural engineers, architects, project managers, design engineers, building inspectors, construction supervisors, real estate developers
Team or Dept this is for: Engineering, Construction, Operations, Project Management, Quality Assurance, Safety, Real Estate Development
Description: Risks of relying on AI-generated blueprints without full validation.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with construction or engineering principles, along with a general awareness of how digital design tools are used in the built environment, is all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with using generative design tools in construction and engineering projects, and explain why AI-generated blueprints must undergo thorough validation before use. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate and verify AI-produced designs, ensuring they meet safety standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices within the built environment.
